news.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
news.summary: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

news.title: What happens when companies become too AI-pilled?
news.summary: The people deciding that AI can replace your job are also the ones least likely to understand what your job truly involves, according to Box founder Aaron Levie, who pointed to this as an example of “AI psychosis.” Indeed, ClickUp recently cut 22% of its workforce for AI agents, tech layoffs i

news.title: After Nvidia’s $20B not-aqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M
news.summary: Chipmaker Groq is looking to raise $650 million in internal funding as it pivots from hardware to focus more on AI inference, the process of refining the way AI models respond to prompted requests, per Axios.

news.title: Microsoft under fire for threatening security researcher with criminal investigation
news.summary: A public spat between Microsoft and an independent security researcher reopens a long-running debate over who is responsible for securing software.

news.title: Cognition’s Scott Wu says AI coding agents shouldn’t replace humans
news.summary: Cognition makes Devin, the first and arguably most successful AI coding agent. But famed coder Wu says it isn't designed to supplant human programmers.

news.title: Acer’s launching a Linux handheld for streaming your PC games
news.summary: The Acer Nitro Blaze Link might run on Linux, but it's no Steam Deck. Acer says it's a "streaming-first handheld and companion device," like a PlayStation Portal for your PC. news.title: Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
news.summary: This week, an AI training startup called Shift said it would clean New Yorkers' homes for free. It has plans to expand into other cities as well, including London, and looking around my flat, I get the appeal. But there's a catch. There's always a catch. news.summary: High school graduation is a time of change that might be felt more deeply by family members than by the grads themselves. news.title: Microsoft delays Fable (again) to avoid GTA VI
news.summary: Microsoft has delayed its upcoming Fable reboot once again. The game was set to launch in autumn 2026, but Microsoft now says that Fable will come out in February 2027. news.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
news.summary: The botnet was reportedly tied to a Russia-based residential proxy network.

news.title: Analysis of Texas measles outbreak shows just how dangerous virus is
news.summary: About 1 in 5 cases were hospitalized and most of those developed complications.

news.summary: ABC says early renewal for all stations is unprecedented, has no legitimate purpose.

news.title: DOJ sues states that rejected ICE requests for undercover license plates
news.summary: DOJ keeps accusing ICE monitoring sites of doxing, but evidence remains scarce.

news.title: Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend
news.summary: Google’s new AI agent combed through my emails, documents, and calendar to plan a birthday party and still didn’t clock the person most important to me.

news.summary: Send the pool guy packing. Let one of these robotic buddies maintain your water quality instead.

news.title: We Asked the ‘Future of Truth’ Author to Explain How He Used AI. It Didn’t Go Well
news.summary: A book about how AI shapes perceptions of reality came under fire for using AI-generated quotes. Its problems go beyond that.

news.title: Samsung Movingstyle Essential Review: A Screen on Wheels
news.summary: Made for those who hate having a TV in the home, Samsung’s Movingstyle monitor-on-wheels brings the entertainment when you need it and hides away in a closet when you don’t.

news.summary: Pope Leo XIV may not be able to disarm AI, but he’s got the attention of the industry.
